_comment: Curated ZSR Library resources. URLs verified against the live ZSR site (zsr.wfu.edu / guides.zsr.wfu.edu) in June 2026 — real HUB pages, not fake deep-links. A librarian should ADD subject-specific databases (JSTOR, PubMed, Business Source, etc.) with the institution's proxied URLs. 'best_for'/'tags'/'keywords' drive retrieval. To make a resource produce clickable 'run this search' links, add a 'search_url_template' with a {q} placeholder (the app URL-encodes the student's search term into it) — e.g. a database or discovery search URL. Add 'search_tool_only': true to keep an entry out of the recommended starting points (used for pure search tools like Google Scholar).

resources.name: ZSR Research Starting Points
resources.description: Use the ZSR site to reach the A-Z Databases list, Subject & Course Research Guides, Find a Journal, ZSR Library Search, and Ask ZSR for topic-specific help.
resources.access: Open to all; databases behind it require WFU login (EZproxy) off campus.

resources.name: A-Z Databases
resources.description: The full, filterable list of ZSR's research databases. Filter by subject or database type to find the best place to search for scholarly articles on your topic.
resources.access: List is open; databases require WFU login (EZproxy) for off-campus access.

resources.name: PsycINFO
resources.description: Psychology database for scholarly literature on adolescent development, mental health, depression, anxiety, behavior, and well-being.

resources.name: Communication & Mass Media Complete
resources.description: Communication and media studies database for media effects, platform use, online behavior, digital culture, and social media research.

resources.name: PubMed / MEDLINE
resources.description: Biomedical and public-health literature for adolescent mental health, screen time, clinical outcomes, and population health research.
resources.access: PubMed is open; full text may require WFU access through ZSR.

resources.name: Subject & Course Research Guides
resources.description: Librarian-built guides recommending the best databases, search strategies, and citation help for a specific subject or course.
best_for: where to start in a field

resources.name: Find a Journal
resources.description: Check whether ZSR provides access to a specific journal or magazine by title, and how to reach it.
resources.access: Searchable by anyone; full text requires WFU login.

resources.name: Special Collections & Archives
resources.description: Rare books, manuscripts, University Archives, and the NC Baptist Historical Collection. The place for original primary-source research.
resources.access: On-site use; contact staff to arrange access.

resources.name: Digital Collections
resources.description: Over 291,000 digitized primary-source items across 124 online collections — available 24/7 with no login.

resources.name: Business Information Commons (Business Research Guide)
resources.description: Guide to business resources — company and industry research, market data, and financial databases — with librarian recommendations.
resources.access: Guide is open; linked databases require WFU login.

resources.name: Citation Managers & Zotero Guide
resources.description: Guidance on citation styles and reference managers, including Zotero (with unlimited file storage for WFU users) to organize sources and generate citations.
resources.access: Open to all; Zotero storage perk requires a wfu.edu account.

resources.name: Interlibrary Loan (ZSR Delivers)
resources.description: Request articles and books ZSR doesn't own from libraries worldwide, usually at no cost to students.
best_for: accessing materials ZSR does not own

resources.name: ZSR Library Search
resources.description: ZSR's main discovery search ('Start your search @ ZSR') — books, articles, and more across the library's collections.
resources.access: Search is open; full text and borrowing require WFU login.

resources.name: Google Scholar
resources.description: Free scholarly search engine. Useful for a quick cross-disciplinary scan; set its library links to Wake Forest to see 'Find it @ WFU' for full text.
best_for: checking if a topic has literature
resources.access: Open to all; full-text access via WFU library links or ZSR.

resources.name: Ask a Librarian (Ask ZSR)
resources.description: Get personalized help from a librarian by form or email (askzsr@wfu.edu), or arrange a research consultation. The best next step when a topic is complex or stuck.
